title: Which of These Four Small Box Games is Right for You?
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=w68xGF_YIgU

Daniel (@dungeondive) runs *Iron Helm*, *Rogue Dungeon*, *Mini Rogue*, and *Unbroken* through a five-category ranking so the viewer can pick which small-box card crawl suits them. The categories: luck-vs-strategy, aesthetics, big-box dungeon-crawl feel, replay value (with expansions in scope), and the catch-all X-factor.

Verdicts by axis. **Luck-vs-strategy:** Mini Rogue (almost pure luck) → Rogue Dungeon → Iron Helm → Unbroken (most strategic). **Aesthetics:** Iron Helm wins on Jason Glover's black-and-white cross-hatched art, Rogue Dungeon last on incoherent direction. **Dungeon-crawl feel:** Rogue Dungeon wins (route choice, loot accretion, stat building), Unbroken last ("feels like balancing a spreadsheet"). **Replay value:** Mini Rogue wins on sheer speed and expansion ecosystem. **X-factor:** Rogue Dungeon takes the overall favourite, Mini Rogue close behind, Iron Helm third ("sometimes feels a little dry"), Unbroken last for being the most mentally taxing.

Daniel's bottom line: all four can coexist on a small shelf without stepping on each other, and Rogue Dungeon is the one he'd keep if forced to pick one. Both Rogue Dungeon and Mini Rogue sit on his Top 50.

----

Daniel ranks Rogue Dungeon over Iron Helm on dungeon-crawl feel but Iron Helm over Rogue Dungeon on art. If you could only have one, which axis is more important for a small-box card crawl?
